subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the main theme of the story and its moral lesson
Identify characters and their traits, especially Lencho
Learn to summarize and analyze events in simple terms
Develop ability to infer emotions, feelings, and reactions of characters
Appreciate the role of faith, hope, and human kindness

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Story
topics_covered: Setting of the story (village, house, and farm)
Introduction to Lencho and his family
teaching_strategy: Use storytelling to introduce the plot
Ask simple questions to gauge prior knowledge of the story
student_activities: Locate the setting on a map of Mexico (optional)
Discuss initial impressions of Lencho as a character
assessment: Ask students who Lencho is and where he lives
Quick oral Q&A on the setting

lesson_plan.title: The Rain and Its Importance
topics_covered: The drought and Lencho’s hope for rain
Impact of rain on the family and community
teaching_strategy: Explain cause and effect: drought → hope → rain
Show images of farms and rain to make the concept clear
student_activities: Draw a simple diagram showing drought and the effect of rain on crops
Discuss what students would do if they were Lencho
assessment: Ask students why rain was important to Lencho
Oral Q&A on the family’s situation

lesson_plan.title: Lencho Writes a Letter
topics_covered: Writing the letter to ask for help
Character traits revealed: trust, faith, hope
teaching_strategy: Explain difficult words and phrases in simple language
student_activities: Students write a few lines imagining they are Lencho
Identify key emotions in Lencho’s letter
assessment: Ask students what Lencho asked for in his letter
Quick written exercise: list 2 traits of Lencho

lesson_plan.title: Response from the Post Office
teaching_strategy: Narrate the post office incident in simple storytelling
student_activities: Role play: post office employees collecting and sending money
Discuss why the employees helped Lencho
assessment: Ask students how the employees helped Lencho
Short oral Q&A on what Lencho received

lesson_plan.title: Lencho’s Reaction and Lesson Learned
topics_covered: Lencho’s reaction to the money received
His faith in God and suspicion of the post office
Moral lesson: faith, hope, human kindness
teaching_strategy: Summarize the story using a simple chart
Highlight the moral in simple language
student_activities: Create a cause-effect chart of the story
Discuss what they learned from Lencho’s story
assessment: Ask students to summarize the story in their own words
Class discussion on lessons learned about hope and kindness

teaching_aids: Images of farms, rain, and letters
Timeline chart of the story events
teacher_tip: Use storytelling and simple language for easy-level students. Encourage students to express emotions and thoughts of characters. Focus on Lencho’s faith, hope, and the human kindness in the story.
